statuses.name: Open
statuses.meaning: New public intake that has not been accepted for roadmap visibility.

statuses.name: Under Review
statuses.meaning: Needs maintainer or product review before acceptance.

statuses.name: Planned
statuses.meaning: Accepted and publicly visible, but not actively in progress.

statuses.name: In Progress
statuses.meaning: Assigned and actively moving toward a concrete release.

statuses.name: Closed
statuses.meaning: Not planned, duplicate, invalid, or handled outside the public roadmap.

categories.description: Start here to understand OpenCoven, CastCodes, Coven, and Coven Code.

categories.name: Products
categories.description: Learn what each public OpenCoven product surface does.

categories.name: Feedback and Roadmaps
categories.description: Use public feedback, product roadmaps, and statuses effectively.

categories.name: Releases and Changelog
categories.description: Follow verified releases, shipped work, and historical milestones.

categories.name: Agents and Safety
categories.description: Understand how agents help with public OpenCoven product work safely.

categories.name: Grimoire
categories.description: Learn OpenCoven language, concepts, and public lore boundaries.

articleRules: Use Title Case for help center article titles.
Keep articles comprehensive enough to answer the user question, but short enough to scan.
Use public-safe language and avoid private infrastructure, secrets, unreleased sensitive context, and raw personal data.

releaseWorkflow: Collect merged PRs, shipped feedback posts, and notable docs changes.
Draft one changelog entry per product release or public milestone.
Link shipped feedback posts to the changelog entry before publishing.
Move linked posts to complete while keeping them on the relevant product roadmap.
Publish the changelog only after the release artifact or deployed change is verified.
agentRules: Do not include secrets, private tokens, private infrastructure URLs, customer-private logs, or raw personal data in public posts, comments, roadmaps, or changelogs.
Summarize private context into public-safe language before posting anything visible.
Use Title Case for public feedback and changelog titles, with short subject-verb-object phrasing where possible.
Use OpenCoven Feedback as the canonical public source of truth; GitHub issues, releases, Discord, and support threads are inputs or announcement channels.
Keep security reports private until Val explicitly approves a sanitized public note.
For duplicate feedback, merge into the highest-signal canonical post and preserve public attribution through comments or linked evidence where safe.
When an item ships, update status, product roadmap membership, and changelog links in the same operating pass.

daily: Review new incoming feedback and obvious duplicates.
Promote clear bugs or feature requests into the right board.
Flag sensitive items for private handling instead of public posting.
weekly: Review product roadmap drift before Weekly Open Coven.
Prepare a short public roadmap update from accepted changes.
Check changelog drafts against shipped artifacts and release notes.
release: Create or update the changelog draft.
Link related posts and roadmap items.
Publish only after verification evidence exists.
